input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule T.4.2.4 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.4.2.4
            TITLE: implausibility is defined as a deviation of more than 10% pedal travel between the sensors or

            CONTENT:
            rule T.4.2.4: Implausibility is defined as a deviation of more than 10% Pedal Travel between the sensors or
T.4.2.4 Implausibility is defined as a deviation of more than 10% Pedal Travel between the sensors or 
other failure as defined in this Section T.4.2.  Use of values larger than 10% Pedal Travel 
require justification in the ETC Systems Form and may not be approved

final_answer: rule T.4.2.4: Implausibility is defined as a deviation of more than 10% Pedal Travel between the sensors or T.4.2.4 Implausibility is defined as a deviation of more than 10% Pedal Travel between the sensors or other failure as defined in this Section T.4.2. Use of values larger than 10% Pedal Travel require justification in the ETC Systems Form and may not be approved
